series.title: Police Squad!
series.description: Created by Zucker, Abrahams and Zucker (Airplane!), Leslie Nielsen stars as the deadpan Det. Lt. Frank Drebin of Police Squad. Only 6 episodes aired before ABC cancelled it — calling it the most stupid reason ever given for cancellation. The series spawned The Naked Gun film trilogy. TV Guide ranked it #7 on its list of 60 shows Cancelled Too Soon.

running_gags.label: Freeze Frame Ending
running_gags.description: Every episode ends with a fake freeze frame — the actors keep moving while the announcer reads credits.

running_gags.label: Johnny the Shoeshine Boy
running_gags.description: The best-connected informant in the city, charging a dollar a shine while dispensing priceless intelligence.

running_gags.label: Ted Olson's Lab
running_gags.description: Ted demonstrates increasingly absurd gadgets and forensic equipment, invariably injuring himself.

running_gags.label: Opening Credits Death
running_gags.description: A celebrity guest star is announced in the opening titles, then immediately killed before they can appear.

running_gags.label: Drebin Narration
running_gags.description: Frank's deadpan noir voiceover states the blindingly obvious or wildly misreads the situation.

running_gags.label: Background Sight Gag
running_gags.description: Visual jokes play out unacknowledged in the background while the foreground scene continues normally.

running_gags.label: Literal Non Sequitur
running_gags.description: Characters take idioms and figures of speech completely literally, or respond to entirely different meanings.

episodes.overview: Lt. Frank Drebin investigates the murder of his partner, Officer Schwartz, who was killed while on a routine stakeout. The trail leads to a criminal enterprise involving a lounge singer and a mysterious gift.

credits_death.fate: Shot while giving an interview, slumps over mid-sentence

freeze_frame.subject: Drebin pouring champagne — the bottle keeps pouring, his arm keeps moving

episodes.johnny_topic: The location of a stolen shipment; reveals it while polishing a shoe, charges one dollar
episodes.lab_scene: Ted demonstrates a new fingerprint analyzer that sprays ink everywhere, covering both him and Drebin
notable_sight_gags: A man is being robbed in the background while Drebin speaks to a witness who ignores it completely
A fire breaks out in a trash can behind the interrogation scene and a janitor calmly extinguishes it
The 'POLICE SQUAD' sign on the door is being installed incorrectly by a workman throughout the scene
episodes.drebin_narration: "It was a quiet Tuesday in the city. The kind of quiet that makes you want to find some noise."

episodes.overview: Drebin goes undercover in the boxing world to investigate a protection racket. When a promising fighter is pressured to throw a match, Frank steps into the ring — literally.

credits_death.fate: Punched through a wall by a boxer during the opening credits sequence

freeze_frame.subject: Drebin throwing a punch at a punching bag — the bag swings back and hits him
freeze_frame.announced_action: Frank trains for the big fight

episodes.johnny_topic: Names the promoter behind the fix while buffing a loafer, pauses dramatically for extra polish
episodes.lab_scene: Ted shows Drebin a new invisible ink system, accidentally writes over all the case files, then cannot find them
notable_sight_gags: A boxer in the background is shadow-boxing himself into a corner and can't find his way out
The gym's weight rack collapses in slow motion behind a tense conversation about nothing
A referee is practicing counting to ten and loses his place repeatedly in the background
episodes.drebin_narration: "The boxing world is a tough place. Almost as tough as the time I investigated the world of competitive pillow fighting."

episodes.overview: A priceless bird goes missing from a wealthy socialite's estate, and the list of suspects includes every single person on the premises. Drebin's interrogation technique produces more non sequiturs than answers.

credits_death.fate: Beamed up mid-sentence, then beamed back down into traffic

freeze_frame.subject: Drebin reaching for a doorknob — his hand freezes inches away while the door swings open on its own
freeze_frame.announced_action: Frank enters the scene of the crime

episodes.johnny_topic: Identifies the bird fence while polishing a riding boot, charges double for exotic footwear
episodes.lab_scene: Ted has developed a bird-call analysis machine that speaks entirely in bird sounds; neither man notices
notable_sight_gags: A butler is polishing silver in the background, including items that are clearly not silver — a banana, a shoe
Two guests at the party are engaged in an escalating mime battle that nobody acknowledges
A maid vacuums the same six inches of carpet in the background for the entire scene
episodes.drebin_narration: "The Butler estate. Old money, old secrets, and carpets so thick you could lose a small dog in them. We'd found three so far."

episodes.overview: A witness with a perfect alibi is the only suspect in a murder case. Drebin must prove the alibi is false, leading him into a world of counterfeiting and identity fraud that mirrors his own confusion about who is who.

credits_death.fate: Impersonates the wrong man and is arrested for being himself

freeze_frame.subject: Drebin caught mid-sneeze — Ed Hocken's toupee is mid-flight

episodes.johnny_topic: Reveals the counterfeiting operation location while resoling a boot; has somehow already known for weeks
episodes.lab_scene: Ted demonstrates a lie detector that measures guilt by shoe size; the results are highly specific to himself
notable_sight_gags: An escalating card game in the background — the players keep adding more cards, chairs, and people
A man is painting a portrait of another man who is painting a portrait of the first man
A window washer appears at increasing story heights throughout the episode, defying the building's actual height
episodes.drebin_narration: "Counterfeiting. The lowest form of crime. Well, maybe the second lowest. I once arrested a man for putting raisins in a chocolate chip cookie."

episodes.overview: The squad investigates a protection racket terrorizing local businesses. Drebin goes undercover as a restaurant owner, producing some of the most literal interpretations of culinary language in television history.

credits_death.fate: Sings one note of a ballad, collapses from a poison dart, finishes the note on the way down

freeze_frame.subject: Drebin mid-karate-chop — a vase is suspended in mid-shatter, petals floating
freeze_frame.announced_action: Frank takes matters into his own hands

episodes.johnny_topic: Names the racket's enforcer while waterproofing a boot; has brought his own lunch and eats it during the conversation
episodes.lab_scene: Ted has created a surveillance camera so small it's invisible — he cannot find it, nor can anyone else; it may not exist
notable_sight_gags: The restaurant's specials board keeps changing between shots to increasingly absurd items
A customer in the background orders, receives, and disputes a bill in complete pantomime over the course of one scene
Two chefs are engaged in a duel with serving utensils that nobody at the tables notices
episodes.drebin_narration: "Big Gulch neighborhood. The kind of place where everybody minds their own business. Except for the guy who minds everyone else's. We called him the Mayor. His name was Gerald."

episodes.overview: The final episode. A key witness to a mob execution refuses to testify — because he's dead, though nobody has told him yet. Drebin must protect a witness, solve a murder, and contend with a courtroom scene of incomparable absurdity.

credits_death.fate: Begins to sing, is interrupted by Drebin requesting he not, obliges, immediately topples over

freeze_frame.subject: The entire courtroom frozen mid-chaos — a gavel is airborne, wigs askew, the judge mid-fall

episodes.johnny_topic: Provides the mob boss's location; has somehow also shined the shoes of everyone in the precinct in advance
episodes.lab_scene: Ted presents evidence in court using a machine that repeats everything he says five seconds later, at increasing volume
notable_sight_gags: The court stenographer types so fast the machine catches fire, which she ignores
The bailiff swears in witnesses using increasingly implausible sacred objects
The jury is clearly asleep, eating, and playing cards — one juror appears to be fishing
episodes.drebin_narration: "They say dead men tell no tales. But Lou Bickerman had a lot to say. We just had to find someone to translate."
